_README: Fast local retrieval eval for NeuroVault. HARDENED: every case's brain is seeded with a shared `distractor_pool` of ~14 realistic, unrelated personal notes PLUS the case's own answer note and topical near-distractors. The query must land its answer within `expect_top` (default 1 = strict top-1). This makes a pass mean genuine ranking quality against a realistic crowd, not 'only note in the brain'. `expect_rank_above` (optional) additionally requires the answer to outrank a named stale/wrong note (current-value cases). Recall-only, no LLM grading => fast + free + laptop-safe. EDIT: replace `cases[].query`/`notes`/`expect_any` with the query shapes YOUR users will actually ask; keep the distractor_pool realistic and non-overlapping with any answer.

distractor_pool.content:
# Dentist

Moved my dental cleaning to Thursday at 3pm; the hygienist said to floss more.

# Food

The new pasta place on 5th does a great cacio e pepe; go early, it fills up.

# Gym

Renewed the gym membership on the annual plan, saves a bit over monthly.

# Reminders

Mom's birthday is in November, need to plan something with my sister.

# Work

Finished the quarterly report and sent it to the team for review before Friday.

# Home

The kitchen faucet is dripping again; need to replace the cartridge this weekend.

# Travel

Booked the December holiday flights; aisle seats both legs this time.

# Listening

The podcast episode on stoicism was surprisingly practical, worth a relisten.

# Phone

Moved to the family phone tier; everyone's lines are on one bill now.

# Garden

The herbs need repotting before spring; basil outgrew its pot.

# Learning

Keeping a daily Duolingo streak going for Spanish; on a 40-day run.

# Meetings

The Monday standup moved to 10am; the design review is now Wednesday afternoon.

# Admin

Home contents insurance renews next month; compare a couple of quotes first.

# Cooking

The sourdough came out better with a longer cold proof, about 18 hours.

cases.why: Durable preference buried in an unrelated narrative note.

notes.content:
# Auth debugging

Spent the morning chasing a token refresh bug in the OAuth flow. While grepping the middleware I remembered I always use ripgrep instead of grep for code search, it's far faster on the monorepo. Then rotated the signing keys.

# Editor setup

Moved my Neovim config to lua, added telescope and treesitter.

cases.query: what do I use for code search

cases.why: Single-fact preference lookup against a crowd.

notes.content:
# Morning routine

I grind Ethiopian Yirgacheffe beans and pull a 1:2 espresso every morning, no milk.

# Tea

Keep a tin of genmaicha at the office for the afternoon.

# Hair care

The lavender shampoo irritated my scalp so I stopped using it. Switched to Trader Joe's tea tree tingle.

# Skin

The new fragrance-free moisturizer is working well.

cases.why: Revised value: current must beat stale.

notes.content:
# Finances (January)

My monthly grocery budget is 400 pounds. I track it in a spreadsheet.

# Finances update (April)

I bumped the grocery budget to 550 pounds after the move.

cases.query: what is my current grocery budget

notes.content:
# Setup (2024)

I use nvim as my main editor for everything.

# Setup update

I switched from nvim to Zed last month, the speed and collab won me over.

cases.query: what editor do I use now

cases.why: Named-person lookup must beat a same-topic distractor.

notes.content:
# Team

Sarah owns the retrieval pipeline and runs the weekly engineering sync on Tuesdays.

# Pipeline

The retrieval pipeline fuses semantic, BM25, and graph signals with RRF.

cases.why: Quantity recall; BGE-small weak on numerals.

notes.content:
# Reading

Finished 'The Nightingale', it was 440 pages and took me two weeks.

# Next up

Started 'Project Hail Mary', currently on chapter 3.

cases.query: how long was the Nightingale book

notes.content:
# Travel

The most recent family trip was to Lisbon in April; we did the tram and the pasteis tour.

# Travel

Earlier in the year we went to the Lake District for a long weekend.

cases.query: where was our most recent family trip

cases.why: The distinct decision must surface over chatter.

notes.content:
# Atlas standup

Status steady, deploy pipeline green, nothing notable.

# Atlas standup

Atlas status unchanged this week, CI stable.

# Atlas decision

We decided to sunset the legacy Atlas API and migrate clients to v2 by Q4.

cases.query: what did we decide about the Atlas API

cases.why: Buried detail must beat same-topic notes lacking it.

notes.content:
# Car

First service on the new Civic was March 15. A week later the GPS head unit failed and the dealer replaced it under warranty.

# Tires

Rotated the tires and topped up the washer fluid.

cases.query: what failed on the new car after its first service
